mission.title: Install KubeStellar Console
mission.description: AI-powered multi-cluster Kubernetes dashboard with guided install missions. This mission securely deploys the console to your current cluster via Helm, ensuring the correct permissions, namespace, and validations are applied.

steps.title: Create Namespace
steps.description:
Create the kubestellar-console namespace if it does not already exist.
```bash
kubectl create namespace kubestellar-console --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
```

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
Add and update the official KubeStellar Helm charts repository.
```bash
helm repo add kubestellar-console https://kubestellar.github.io/console
helm repo update kubestellar-console
```

steps.title: Deploy via Helm
steps.description:
Deploy the console chart pointing to the dedicated namespace, keeping default values unless configured.
```bash
helm install kc kubestellar-console/kubestellar-console --namespace kubestellar-console --wait --timeout 120s
```

steps.title: Verify Deployment Status
steps.description:
Ensure the replica pods are healthy and not locked in CrashLoopBackOff.
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=kc -n kubestellar-console --timeout=90s
```

resolution.summary: The KubeStellar Console is successfully installed. To access it locally, run `kubectl port-forward -n kubestellar-console svc/kc-kubestellar-console 8080:8080` and open http://localhost:8080. Consider configuring AI provider keys in your environment variables prior to start so that your console instances can utilize advanced Agentic LLMs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Console
uninstall.description:
Remove the helm release alongside its scoped namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall kc -n kubestellar-console
kubectl delete namespace kubestellar-console
```

upgrade.title: Update Resources
upgrade.description:
Update the latest helm charts and apply them.
```bash
helm repo update kubestellar-console
helm upgrade kc kubestellar-console/kubestellar-console -n kubestellar-console --wait
```

troubleshooting.title: Check Pod Logs
troubleshooting.description:
If your console pods fail to reach a running state, review logging for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=kc -n kubestellar-console
```

prerequisites.description: You need a functioning Kubeconfig tied to an accessible cluster.
